frameworks.name: fMRI multivariate pattern decoding
frameworks.one_line: linear classifiers trained within/across features to test independence of neural codes

frameworks.name: orthogonal independent feature coding
frameworks.one_line: shape and color represented in separable, non-interacting subspaces of OTC

frameworks.name: interactive feature binding in working memory
frameworks.one_line: delay-period representations integrate shape and color for efficient storage

frameworks.name: perception-to-VWM transformation
frameworks.one_line: sensory representations are reformatted to meet working-memory demands

core_question: How are an object's shape and color jointly represented in human occipitotemporal cortex during visual working memory?
key_assumptions: cross-decoding drop indexes interactive (non-independent) feature coding
OTC shape-selective and ventral color areas index relevant feature representations
VWM delay activity reflects maintained content
manipulations: task phase: VWM encoding vs delay
decoding scheme: within-color vs cross-color shape decoding
object features held in memory: shape paired with color
key_findings: encoding showed significant cross-decoding with no drop, indicating independent coding
delay also showed significant cross-decoding overall
delay produced a cross-decoding drop in OTC shape-selective and ventral color areas
sensory shape and color representations are transformed during VWM delay
interpretations: shape and color become interactively coded in OTC during VWM delay
transformation likely supports more efficient information storage in VWM
